question_title: Will OFAC publish at least one Belarus-related sanctions action before May 1, 2026?
summary: OFAC has been actively modifying Belarus sanctions in late 2025/early 2026, with 4 distinct actions in the last 6 months including a major March 26, 2026 action (SDN removals, GL 14, Directive 1 rescission). The question asks about ANY Belarus-related action in April 2026 under a very broad definition (designations, GLs, FAQs, regulations, or other actions). The March 26 actions likely require Federal Register publications that could be dated in April. The recent pace of activity (~67% monthly rate) far exceeds the long-term base rate (~22%). The main risk is that the comprehensive March 26 action represents a capstone reducing further April activity. Combining the probability of FR publications in April (~65%) with the probability of new standalone actions (~40%), I estimate ~75% probability of at least one qualifying action.

factors.description: Very active recent Belarus sanctions modification campaign: 4 actions in the last 6 months (Oct 2025-Mar 2026), including major March 26 action with SDN removals, GL 14, and Directive rescission. The administration is clearly engaged in an ongoing Belarus sanctions easing program.

Federal Register publications for the March 26 actions likely to be dated in April. The March 26 OFAC action included SDN removals, GL 14, and Directive 1 rescission — each typically requires a Federal Register notice published days to weeks later.

Very broad definition of qualifying actions: designations, general licenses, FAQs, regulations, or 'other OFAC actions' listed as Belarus-related. Even a simple FAQ update or guidance clarification would count.
Long-term base rate is only ~22% (8 months out of ~37 had actions), suggesting many months pass without any Belarus action. This dampens the high recent rate.
March 26 action was comprehensive (removals + GL + Directive rescission). This could represent a capstone that reduces need for further action in April. The easing campaign may pause.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.75 (75% probability YES)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Active easing campaign**: The Trump administration has been actively modifying Belarus sanctions, with 4 actions in just the last 6 months. The March 26, 2026 action was a major package (SDN removals of Belaruskali/BPC, General License 14, Directive 1 rescission).

- **Federal Register pipeline**: The March 26 multi-component action typically generates Federal Register notices published days-to-weeks later, which could carry April dates and would count as qualifying actions.

- **Broad definition helps YES**: The fine print counts "designations, general licenses, FAQs, regulations, or other OFAC actions" — even minor administrative follow-ups would qualify.

- **Main downside risk**: The long-term base rate is only ~22% of months having any Belarus action, and the comprehensive March 26 action might represent a natural pause point in the easing campaign.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will OFAC publish at least one Belarus-related sanctions action before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42927)
[Belarus Designations Removals; Issuance of Belarus General License; Rescission of Belarus Directive 1](https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions/20260326)
[OFAC Sanctions: Top 5 Trends for 2026 | Insights | Holland & Knight](https://www.hklaw.com/en/insights/publications/2026/01/ofac-sanctions-top-5-trends-for-2026)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if OFAC publishes at least one Belarus-related sanctions action dated from April 1, 2026 through April 30, 2026. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is [https://ofac.treasury.gov/sanctions-programs-and-country-information/belarus-sanctions](https://ofac.treasury.gov/sanctions-programs-and-country-information/belarus-sanctions)
fine_print: Count designations, general licenses, FAQs, regulations, or other OFAC actions listed as Belarus-related. Exclude actions dated before April 1, 2026 even if they remain visible on the page. Candidate Sources (for backup): OFAC Recent Actions page [https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions](https://ofac.treasury.gov/recent-actions)
